title: Cordia dasycephala
taxonomy: Cordia dasycephala is a species within the Apocynaceae family, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, with Cordia dasycephala being part of the Apocynoideae subfamily. The Apocynaceae family is native to various parts of the world, including tropical and subtropical regions. The Apocynoideae subfamily is characterized by its unique characteristics, such as having paired leaves and showy flowers. Cordia dasycephala's classification is consistent with the broader classification of the Apocynaceae family.
uses: Cordia dasycephala has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits. Its leaves and other parts are sometimes used to treat various ailments, including fever and digestive issues. In some cultures, it is used to make a tea that is believed to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Additionally, Cordia dasycephala has been studied for its potential antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. More research is needed to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential benefits to human health.
benefits: Cordia dasycephala, also known as the 'Cordia' or 'Wild Plum', is a plant species that is rich in various bioactive compounds. constituents: Cordia dasycephala contains alkaloids, which are active constituents in the plant that have been studied for their potential medicinal properties. These alkaloids, including cordia dimer A and cordia dimer B, have shown antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities in laboratory studies. parts: Cordia dasycephala has been traditionally used in folk medicine in various parts of the world. The medicinal parts of this plant are primarily its leaves and roots. preparations: Cordia dasycephala is used in traditional medicine, and herbal preparations are made from its leaves and other parts. The leaves are used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. In some regions, a decoction of the leaves is consumed to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
